name: Heavenly Cream Cake
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch, 3/11/96
1. In a 1 1/2-quart saucepan, melt margarine over medium-low heat.
2. Add milk, cocoa powder, and vanilla; stir briskly with a wire whisk until the mixture comes to a boil.
3. Remove from heat; stir in powdered sugar. Stir with a wire whisk for about 1 minute, until the sauce is fairly smooth (you will have a few small lumps).
4. Place cake on a cake plate that has a cover. With a long, sharp, serrated knife, slice the cake into 3 equal horizontal layers.
5. Gently remove the top 2 layers and set aside.
6. Spread 1 2/3 containers of pudding on the bottom layer (over the section where it was cut) to form a filling.
7. Top with the second cake layer; spread on the remaining 1 1/3 containers pudding.
8. Top with the remaining layer.
9. Pour chocolate sauce in a thin stream directly on top of the cake, allowing it to run down the sides and cover the cake almost entirely (there will be quite a bit of sauce on the plate).
10. Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
11. To serve: Cut into slices with a sharp, serrated knife using a sawing motion. Serve extra sauce from the plate along with the slices, if desired. Serve at once.
12. Yield: 8 servings
